problem_statement: Python control-plane receipt claims reference the local MAF Rust substrate, but the current runtime does not call Rust. A typed witness is needed to record hash parity evidence, crate-surface mappings, and F8 gaps without granting runtime binding authority.
success_criteria: MafReceiptParityWitness validates against a public JSON schema and Foundation Mode example
MafReceiptParityWitness records Python/Rust receipt-hash and state-hash parity constants from local tests
MafReceiptParityWitness maps all local MAF Rust workspace crates to Python or schema surfaces with explicit gap refs

MafReceiptParityWitness blocks PyO3, Python Rust imports, Rust subprocess invocation, MAF CLI invocation, network calls, filesystem mutation, receipt-emission mutation, runtime certification claims, Rust-certifies-Python claims, production binding, terminal closure, and success claims
MafReceiptParityWitness is covered by a focused validator, root test, protocol manifest entry, proof coverage matrix entry, and CI hook
non_goals: No PyO3, subprocess, MAF CLI, or runtime Rust binding authority
no Python import from a Rust runtime module
no Rust subprocess or CLI execution
constraints: MAF Rust workspace inspection remains read-only
hash parity remains test-time evidence only
all runtime authority flags remain false
all gap refs remain explicit until operator tooling and ABI decisions exist
